summary:
The dominant story is the **Ethereum Foundation governance meltdown**: Laura Shin's Unchained podcast with **Zak Cole** and **Greg Markou** reveals Tomasz Stańczak was installed without real power and pushed out for trying to modernize; the EF is now funding "longevity research and hippos in Asia" while a mysterious replacement no one has met runs it.

**Steve Hou** publicly updates his 2023 "baby AI bubble" thesis, now aligning with **@GavinSBaker** and **@tszzl**'s no-bubble view — arguing ROI from AI tokens will materialize in consumer/enterprise apps with current model intelligence levels, and flagging the market's failure to price an "inference explosion" from cheap non-frontier LLMs embedded in everything.

**Macro:** **Ed Bradford** sees front-end UST supply and futures rolls dominating flows this week as the Iran deal buoys rates, with **30-year yields back at 5%**. **Bob Elliott** warns housing turned down in the March Case-Shiller index before the yield spike — suggesting weakness is not rate-driven alone. Taiwan chip output growth slowed to **11% y/y** (from 26%) while Chinese semi output accelerated to 22%. **Quinn Thompson** holds higher-for-longer oil is structural regardless of near-term Iran War resolution.

In equities and crypto, **Micron ($MU)** hits **$1T market cap** (from $80 to $887) as a generational AI memory trade. **$HYPE** continues to dominate YTD crypto performance vs BTC/ETH/SOL. **Simon Dedic** flags the **$DEUS TGE tomorrow** with aligned conditions: robotics crypto theme hot, $BOT at massive premium to NAV, alts catching bids. **Stacy Muur** breaks down Citrea's CTR launch — first vote-escrow model in Bitcoin ecosystem, $18M pre-deposit vault filling in minutes.

**Contrarian signals:** **Udi Wertheimer** argues ZEC privacy hype has almost nothing to do with actual privacy demand, and Bitcoin privacy itself is "pretty weak." **Evanss6** warns Hyperliquid's "community tokens" will never be released. **Nic Carter** pushes back on fiscal hawks: the US has a spending problem, not a revenue problem — effective household tax rates have held at their 1979-present average.

A_events.cluster: Ethereum Foundation Governance Crisis — EF Exposed as Out of Touch

items.author: laurashin
items.summary: Laura Shin runs extensive podcast clips from Zak Cole and Greg Markou on the EF's dysfunction. Zak: the community pressured EF to bring in Tomasz, but "they didn't really want to cede any control" and "never gave him any real power"; when he tried to push real change, "he got run out" for not conforming to "infinite garden nonsense." He was replaced by "a mysterious fellow nobody even knows and has never met." Zak's verdict: EF is "completely out of touch," funding "hippos in Asia" and longevity research nobody cares about; Ethereum is no longer a startup and has "trillions of dollars on the line." Bottom line quote: "Let's grow up and actually run Ethereum like an organization that can scale — or the crypto principles have no real world value." Greg Markou says the wave of senior EF departures is systematic, not budgetary, and the board needs "grownups in the room" who understand businesses at scale.

items.author: laurashin
items.summary: Podcast landing tweet: @0xzak argues Vitalik created the very ETH bear cases the EF now contends with; @gregthegreek says the wave of senior departures isn't budgetary, it's systematic. Show is live on Unchained.

items.author: hosseeb
items.summary: Haseeb announces this week's Chopping Block episode will feature @TrustlessState and @MaxResnick on "ETH apostates and the state of the EF" — asks audience what to ask them, signaling continued institutional interest in the EF dysfunction narrative.

A_events.cluster: Steve Hou Updates AI View — From Baby Bubble to No-Bubble Adjacent

items.author: stevehou
items.summary: Steve Hou publicly updates his 2023 "baby bubble" AI thesis, now saying he's "much closer to @GavinSBaker and @tszzl, who both said we weren't ready for the scenario where there's no bubble." He believes ROI from AI tokens will start showing up in consumer and enterprise applications even at current SOTA intelligence levels, especially as open source catches up. Separately argues the market is not pricing an "inference explosion" — non-frontier, extremely cheap LLMs being incorporated into "every single thing you can think of and not think of in society."

items.author: fejau_inc
items.summary: Responding to Steve Hou's AI bull update: "As long as the Taiwan old guard keep supply tight" — flagging chip supply discipline as the key variable sustaining the no-bubble AI investment thesis. Separately notes Anthropic is now EBIT positive.

items.author: aleabitoreddit
items.summary: Serenity marks $MU hitting a $1T market cap after going from $80 to $887. Notes the prior thesis — "$MU is the next $NVDA given how memory demand looks structural with AI" — has played out. Analogizes to early Nvidia at $400B when markets dismissed GPUs as a short-term cycle. Cites White House backing and "Made in America" tailwind as additional structural support. The stock "probably made a lot of millionaires."

A_events.cluster: DeepSWE — New AI Coding Benchmark Endorsed

items.author: garrytan
items.summary: Garry Tan endorses DeepSWE as "the new standard for engineering evals." Key insight in quoted content: top models look relatively close on public leaderboards, but DeepSWE shows where they actually diverge, reflecting realistic developer day-to-day experience. Significant for anyone benchmarking AI coding tools.

A_events.cluster: Helius Indexes Full Solana Transfer History Since Genesis

items.author: mert
items.summary: Helius (mert's company) has indexed every single token transfer in Solana's history since genesis — more data than all other blockchains combined. New `getTransfersByAddress` endpoint supports full-history analysis with filters per token (amounts, senders, receivers, dates) across any stablecoin, memecoin, or token. Thread includes link to docs and Claude/Clanker integration suggestion for developers.

items.author: mert
items.summary: Separately defends Solana's builder moat vs @CryptoKaleo: "it's the most usable, scalable, and battle-tested crypto app platform in existence" — noting disproportionate share of big crypto startups (pump, axiom, jupiter, phantom, jito, prop AMMs) all started on Solana. Not an accident.

A_events.cluster: Polymarket/Kalshi Banned in Spain — Regulatory Pressure Mounts

items.author: stacy_muur
items.summary: Spain blocks Polymarket and Kalshi via ISP restrictions after regulators found the platforms operating unlicensed betting products. Stacy Muur raises a sharp rhetorical challenge: how can Polymarket claim to be the "leading global prediction market" if they keep getting banned in a new country every month? Regulatory overhang increasingly material.

A_events.cluster: $DEUS TGE Tomorrow — Robotics Crypto Theme Aligned

items.author: sjdedic
items.summary: Simon Dedic flags the $DEUS TGE tomorrow for XMAQUINA — described as "the only crypto project giving direct real-world robotics exposure to some of the most prestigious humanoid companies in the world." Three conditions aligned: (1) RoboStrategy's $BOT launched and is already trading at a massive premium to NAV, proving concept interest; (2) crypto market waking up, quality alts catching bids; (3) robotics-related coins outperforming. "All eyes on $DEUS."

B_insights.author: stevehou
B_insights.summary: The market is not pricing an "inference explosion": non-frontier, extremely cheap LLM models being incorporated into every single thing in society — "I mean every single thing you can think of and not think of." The real underpriced scenario is not which frontier model wins, but pervasive deployment of cheap models across all applications. This is a distinct bull case from the SOTA/capex thesis.

B_insights.author: Pentosh1
B_insights.summary: Extended bull thesis for a 10-year "Golden Dragon Bull Run" across AI, robotics, medicine, defense (AI drones), and energy — with many shakeouts and peaks and valleys along the way. Key insight: every new release is the worst version we will ever see of these technologies; humans think linearly but the pace is logarithmic. "The biggest mistake is looking at things on too low of a time frame." Scaling remains the binding constraint for now but not permanently. Separately warns: crypto AI is "90% just memes parading as AI" — real AI is up because it has products, revenues, pipelines, customers, and an estimated $7.6T in capex over the next 4 years.

B_insights.author: Pentosh1
B_insights.summary: Animal spirits thread: COVID-era stimmies and lockdowns created a generation of speculative capital that is still working through cycles. The crypto sector saw 32x returns in 2-3 months on some assets; prediction markets, 0DTE, sports betting now provide alternative outlets for the same behavior. Crypto will still have boom-bust cycles but not to prior degree — it's "flawed by design with emissions and no longer starting from 0" (token emissions at 2 recent peaks were $500M/day). Advice: "be like an animal that migrates with the seasons in search of food" — don't stare at an empty bowl. Expects 2-3 distinct massive bubbles across different sectors in the next decade.

B_insights.author: Pentosh1
B_insights.summary: Added more BlackBerry (QNX) today and plans to continue. Bull case: CEO says the company is no longer "in transition" but in a profitable growth phase; 5% stock buyback; stated rule of 40; embedded in 275M vehicles; expanding into robotics, defense, and medical applications; "digital sovereignty tailwind" as non-US governments seek secure, US-independent communications; QNX-powered humanoid robot demo just shown; NVDA partnership integrated with THOR/Halo safety stack. Sees "immense long-term upside" as a multi-year play.

B_insights.author: qthomp
B_insights.summary: Oil continues to chop bulls and bears: Brent-WTI spread and curve time spreads have both compressed as SPR releases alleviated physical shortages. However, Quinn Thompson's core thesis remains intact — regardless of how the Iran War resolves near-term, higher-for-longer oil prices are here to stay and international oil & gas equities remain attractive. Equity investors can look through short-term flow dynamics.

B_insights.author: Fullcarry
B_insights.summary: Front-end UST supply and futures rolls (M to U) will dominate trading flows this week. The Iran deal is buoying USTs from what Bradford calls "the depths of despair" — 30-year yields are back to 5%. Context: Conference Board labor market gauge shows not much improvement, a soft data overhang that may cap how much rate relief extends.

B_insights.author: BobEUnlimited
B_insights.summary: March Case-Shiller housing price index turned back negative before the recent spike in long-end yields. This is a bearish data point: housing weakness is not purely a rate story — it was already rolling over when yields were lower. Suggests more structural demand issues in housing.

B_insights.author: BobEUnlimited
B_insights.summary: Flags a potential semis slowdown: Taiwan's April manufacturing data shows chip output growth slowed to 11% y/y (was 26%), the shallowest rate in over 2 years. Meanwhile Chinese semiconductor output accelerated 22%, outpacing Taiwan. The US is currently 10% short. Bob frames this as a question: are we seeing signs of a slowdown in semis?

B_insights.author: udiWertheimer
B_insights.summary: Bitcoin privacy is "pretty weak" in practice. Most promoted mitigations are "flat-out bad." Anyone whose life depends on privacy would need to expertly analyze their own threat model and build a custom solution — ordinary people are better off using something else entirely. The more important signal: current ZEC hype "has very little to do with actual desire for privacy" — the biggest ZEC proponents hold exposure via Coinbase and Hyperliquid, signaling this is a narrative trade rather than a use-case driven one.

B_insights.author: patrick_oshag
B_insights.summary: Patrick OShaughnessy shares extended quote from Darren Farber (defense investor, former DoD special advisor) on the Ukraine lesson: "The rate of technological change in the commercial world becomes a direct input into military capability." Drones existed since Vietnam but only became mass warfare tools when commercial viability made them cheap — 50 iterations of Ukrainian drones in 3 years. "Things that become permissive and inexpensive in the commercial world have enormously valuable inputs into fighting asymmetric war." The commercial capex cycle and military modernization are now tightly linked.

B_insights.author: himgajria
B_insights.summary: Observes extraordinary retail wealth creation happening outside the typical fintech-bubble observer's frame: people making $200k/day on stocks with portfolios under $1M. Interprets this as a macro signal about cycle positioning. Follow-on: crypto's degen phase was 2024-2025; now only fundamentally backed assets are moving — cites HYPE and NEAR as the current quality plays.

B_insights.author: nic_carter
B_insights.summary: Responding to @TomSteyer/@JeffBezos on fiscal policy: the effective US household tax rate is at its long-term average for the entire 1979-present data period — this is not a low-tax era. Government spending is "through the roof." If you strip out government subsidies back to households, the effective tax rate is actually at the high end of its historical range. The US has a spending problem, not a revenue problem. Provides two charts as evidence.

B_insights.author: zriboua
B_insights.summary: Flags the Quad summit/declaration as "a very important development" while Operation Epic Fury (US-led Iran operations) is ongoing. The Quad — representing 1/3 of world GDP and nearly 2B people — is positioning itself for "bold and innovative action." Context: simultaneous military operation and alignment of major democratic powers signals a coordinated geopolitical response to Iranian threats.

B_insights.author: stacy_muur
B_insights.summary: Deep-dive thread on Citrea's CTR token launch. Citrea is implementing the first vote-escrow model in the Bitcoin ecosystem. CTR is live on major exchanges; 10B fixed supply, 12% for genesis distributions. The ctUSD pre-deposit vault's original $15M cap filled in seconds and was raised to $18M — also filled in under 5 minutes, with institutional liquidity commitments. Mechanics: staking CTR converts to xCTR (non-transferable), which votes on the gauge system directing liquidity rewards; stakers earn boosted emissions on pools they back plus bribes from competing projects. Author discloses being a long-time Citrea supporter and depositor who got in early.

B_insights.author: fejau_inc
B_insights.summary: Notes in passing that Anthropic is now EBIT positive — a significant milestone for an AI lab burning capital at scale. Relevant as context for the no-bubble AI thesis: if AI labs are reaching profitability, the ROI narrative becomes more credible.

C_contrarian.author: udiWertheimer
C_contrarian.summary: AGAINST ZEC PRIVACY NARRATIVE: The current Zcash hype has almost nothing to do with actual desire for financial privacy. The largest ZEC proponents hold exposure via Coinbase and Hyperliquid — platforms that are the antithesis of privacy. This is a narrative trade, not a use-case driven one. Simultaneously, Bitcoin's privacy is also weak and most promoted mitigations are bad — the entire crypto privacy narrative is oversold.

C_contrarian.author: Pentosh1
C_contrarian.summary: AGAINST CRYPTO AI HYPE: 90% of crypto AI projects are "memes parading as AI." Real AI (equities) is performing because it has products, revenues, customer pipelines, and an estimated $7.6T in capex over the next 4 years. Crypto AI projects lack all of this. Expects "some good AI projects will come out" but warns against conflating the crypto AI meme cycle with the genuine AI infrastructure buildout.

C_contrarian.author: stevehou
C_contrarian.summary: SELF-DESCRIBED VIEW UPDATE — AI NO-BUBBLE THESIS GAINING: Steve Hou had called AI a "baby bubble" since early 2023 and invested accordingly. He is now publicly updating, saying he's "practically much closer" to the @GavinSBaker/@tszzl no-bubble camp. Noteworthy as a bear-to-bull update from a fund manager who was publicly positioned against the consensus.

C_contrarian.author: stacy_muur
C_contrarian.summary: AGAINST POLYMARKET BRAND NARRATIVE: Challenges the "leading global prediction market" claim by pointing out the contradiction: a platform that gets banned in a new country every month cannot coherently claim global leadership. The Spain/Kalshi ban highlights a structural regulatory vulnerability that the prediction market bull narrative underweights.

C_contrarian.author: nic_carter
C_contrarian.summary: AGAINST MAINSTREAM FISCAL NARRATIVE: Pushes back on the "tax cuts for the rich" framing dominant in media. Data: effective US household tax rates have been at their 1979-2026 average throughout; net of subsidies back to households, tax burden is at the high end of historical range. The problem is a spending explosion, not a revenue shortfall. Two charts provided as evidence.

C_contrarian.author: Evan_ss6
C_contrarian.summary: AGAINST HYPERLIQUID COMMUNITY TOKEN UNLOCK THESIS: Warns that those banking on Hyperliquid's "community tokens" being released are going to be disappointed — "wait til they realize those community tokens will never be released." A minority view given the broadly bullish HYPE positioning seen elsewhere in this feed.

C_contrarian.author: himgajria
C_contrarian.summary: AGAINST CRYPTO RE-ENTRY TIMING: Crypto's degen phase for this cycle was 2024-2025 and is over. What moves now are fundamentally backed assets (citing HYPE and NEAR). The implication: those expecting a broad speculative crypto revival similar to 2021 are late-cycle or wrong. Quality-only positioning going forward.

D_positioning.author: Pentosh1
D_positioning.summary: LONG BlackBerry (QNX) — added more today, plans to continue buying. Multi-year thesis: profitable growth phase, 5% buyback, rule of 40, in 275M vehicles, expanding to robotics/defense/medical, digital sovereignty tailwind, NVDA partnership (THOR/Halo safety stack), QNX-powered humanoid demo. Sees "immense long-term upside."

D_positioning.author: qthomp
D_positioning.summary: LONG International Oil & Gas Equities — higher-for-longer oil is structural thesis. SPR releases have relieved physical shortages and compressed Brent-WTI spread short-term, but this creates an entry opportunity for equity investors who can look through short-term flow dynamics. Iran War resolution does not change the structural case.

D_positioning.author: aleabitoreddit
D_positioning.summary: BULLISH $MU — thesis has played out ($80 to $887, $1T market cap) but Serenity frames this as validation of the structural AI memory demand thesis, analogous to early Nvidia at $400B. White House manufacturing support and structural HBM demand from AI inference remain as forward tailwinds.

D_positioning.author: Crypto_McKenna
D_positioning.summary: LONG $HYPE (contextual) — posts YTD performance chart of $HYPE versus BTC, ETH, and SOL. Visual evidence of HYPE's dominance as the cycle's standout performer among major crypto assets.

D_positioning.author: sjdedic
D_positioning.summary: LONG $DEUS (TGE TOMORROW) — Simon Dedic sees timing as ideal: RoboStrategy's $BOT trading at massive premium to NAV validates robotics crypto demand; crypto market recovering with quality alts catching bids; robotics coins outperforming. XMAQUINA is positioned as the only direct crypto exposure to real-world humanoid robotics companies.

D_positioning.author: himgajria
D_positioning.summary: LONG Quality Crypto (HYPE, NEAR) — explicitly citing these as the only fundamentally backed assets moving in this phase of the cycle. Crypto's degen moment was 2024-2025; what works now is quality with real fundamentals behind it.

D_positioning.author: Evan_ss6
D_positioning.summary: SHORT/SKEPTICAL HYPE COMMUNITY TOKEN UNLOCK — warns the anticipated release of Hyperliquid's community token allocation "will never happen," implying anyone long on that catalyst will be disappointed. Indirect short signal on a specific HYPE narrative.

D_positioning.author: stacy_muur
D_positioning.summary: LONG Citrea CTR/ctUSD — author discloses getting in early on ctUSD pre-deposit vault (filled in seconds/minutes, raised from $15M to $18M cap). CTR now live on major exchanges. Thesis: first vote-escrow model in Bitcoin ecosystem, institutional liquidity commitments, xCTR governance drives emissions to backing pools.

D_positioning.author: MoonOverlord
D_positioning.summary: IMPLICITLY BULLISH $HYPE — responding to someone defending a trader who is short $100M of HYPE: "Is short $100M of the best coin released in the last 5 years. Are we sure he knows lol." Reflects the strong conviction in HYPE bulls that this short is a losing position.

E_links.author: mert
E_links.description: Helius getTransfersByAddress API docs — now supports full Solana transfer history since genesis with per-token filters. Relevant for on-chain analytics and developer tooling.

E_links.author: spotgamma
E_links.description: SpotGamma free webinar 'Playing Against the House' — Wednesday 5/27 at 8pm ET. Topic: using key gamma levels to identify where momentum stalls or reverses when major news drives markets.

E_links.author: stacy_muur
E_links.description: Citrea CTR airdrop claim portal — eligible users can claim and choose to stake.

E_links.author: stacy_muur
E_links.description: Citrea CTR staking portal — stake CTR to receive xCTR and vote on gauge system directing liquidity rewards. 10M CTR distributed to early stakers.

E_links.author: laurashin
E_links.description: Unchained podcast episode: Zak Cole and Greg Markou on the Ethereum Foundation governance crisis — Tomasz departure, systematic leadership failure, what EF needs to do to survive. Multiple tweet excerpts in main feed.

E_links.author: patrick_oshag
E_links.description: Invest Like the Best podcast — second conversation with Darren Farber (Albion River, former DoD special advisor) on what winning means in a theater like Iran and the commercial tech → military capability transfer thesis.

E_links.author: Fullcarry
E_links.description: Conference Board consumer confidence / labor market gauge chart — not much improvement in the latest reading. Soft data overhang for USTs.
